methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Rainmaker
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Dronamics
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Intramotev
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Seneca is a Series A-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Stuart Landesberg and Nick Foley that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of eight private-market peers in drones and autonomous systems, including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

The stage-progression method is the applicable valuation approach given the data constraints. Using total capital raised ($60M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage advancement yields an implied post-money valuation of $120M. This reflects the expected revaluation from seed/Series A entry to early growth stage. A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow drones and autonomous systems category; widening the peer set would introduce material sector heterogeneity and reduce analytical precision.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $102M (low) to $138M (high), centered on $120M post-money. The median reflects the stage-progression result. The primary uncertainty stems from reliance on a single methodological input and the thinness of same-stage peer data in frontier-tech autonomous systems. Until Seneca's unit economics, pathway to revenue, or customer concentration become clearer, or until more directly comparable late-stage funding events materialize, movement outside this $102M–$138M band would require material new information on either company fundamentals or market conditions.
